test_cases.title: Android backup/ADB data exfiltration
test_cases.description: Create logical backups or use ADB to pull app-private data, checking for sensitive information stored outside sandbox controls.
steps: Enable developer mode/USB debugging on the test device or emulator.
Run `adb backup -noapk <package>` or `adb pull /data/data/<package>` (rooted) to acquire app data.
Use Android Backup Extractor / sqlite3 to inspect preferences, databases, and cached tokens.
Report plaintext secrets and recommend encrypted storage with KeyStore.

test_cases.title: Android/iOS SSL pinning bypass validation
test_cases.description: Validate whether SSL/TLS pinning can be bypassed via Frida/Objection hooks, indicating weak certificate validation.
steps: Capture baseline traffic through Burp to confirm pinning/handshake failures.
Deploy Frida scripts or use Objection (`objection --gadget <pkg> explore --sslpinning disable`) to hook validation APIs.
Re-test traffic interception; success indicates pinning can be bypassed by malware/rooted users.
Recommend hardware-backed key attestation or multi-point validation.

test_cases.title: Jailbreak/root detection bypass
test_cases.description: Assess the robustness of jailbreak/root checks and whether they can be bypassed to unlock hidden functionality.
steps: Trigger jailbreak/root detection by launching on a rooted/jailbroken device and note blocked actions.
Use Frida/Objection to hook detection methods (file checks, system property reads, JNI calls).
Demonstrate access to restricted features once bypassed and document the risk.
Recommend server-side attestation (SafetyNet/DeviceCheck) and layered detection mechanisms.
